The **2018 [European Figure Skating Championships](/wiki/European_Figure_Skating_Championships "European Figure Skating Championships")** were held in January 2018 in [Moscow](/wiki/Moscow "Moscow"), [Russia](/wiki/Russia "Russia").[[1]](#cite_note-ISU140615-1) Medals were awarded in the disciplines of [men's singles, ladies' singles](/wiki/Single_skating "Single skating"), [pairs](/wiki/Pair_skating "Pair skating"), and [ice dance](/wiki/Ice_dance "Ice dance").

## Records

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2018_European_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Records")]

See also: [List of highest historical scores in figure skating](/wiki/List_of_highest_historical_scores_in_figure_skating "List of highest historical scores in figure skating")

The following new [ISU best scores](/wiki/ISU_Judging_System#Best_scores "ISU Judging System") were set during this competition:

| Event | Component | Skater(s) | Score | Date | Ref |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Ice dance | Free dance | [France](/wiki/France "France") [Gabriella Papadakis](/wiki/Gabriella_Papadakis "Gabriella Papadakis") / [Guillaume Cizeron](/wiki/Guillaume_Cizeron "Guillaume Cizeron") | **121.87** | 20 January 2018 | [[2]](#cite_note-isu-progression-D-FD-2) |
| Total score | **203.16** | [[3]](#cite_note-isu-progression-D-TO-3) |

## Eligibility

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2018_European_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Eligibility")]

Skaters were eligible for the event if they represented a European [member nation](/wiki/International_figure_skating "International figure skating") of the [International Skating Union](/wiki/International_Skating_Union "International Skating Union") and had reached the age of 15 before July 1, 2017, in their place of birth. The corresponding competition for non-European skaters is the [2018 Four Continents Championships](/wiki/2018_Four_Continents_Figure_Skating_Championships "2018 Four Continents Figure Skating Championships"). National associations selected their entries according to their own criteria but the ISU mandated that their selections achieve a minimum technical elements score (TES) at an international event prior to the European Championships.

### Minimum TES

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2018_European_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Minimum TES")]

The ISU stipulates that the minimum scores must be achieved at an ISU-recognized senior international competition in the ongoing or preceding season, no later than 21 days before the first official practice day.[[4]](#cite_note-Announce-4)

| Minimum technical scores (TES)[[4]](#cite_note-Announce-4) | | |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Discipline | [SP](/wiki/Short_program_(figure_skating) "Short program (figure skating)") / [SD](/wiki/Short_dance "Short dance") | [FS](/wiki/Free_skating "Free skating") / [FD](/wiki/Free_dance_(ice_dance) "Free dance (ice dance)") |
| Men | 25 | 45 |
| Ladies | 20 | 36 |
| Pairs | 20 | 36 |
| Ice dance | 19 | 29 |
| SP and FS scores may be attained at different events. | | |

### Number of entries per discipline

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2018_European_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Number of entries per discipline")]

Based on the results of the [2017 European Championships](/wiki/2017_European_Figure_Skating_Championships "2017 European Figure Skating Championships"), the ISU allows each country one to three entries per discipline.
